channel.title: KnowingSemioverse on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in KnowingSemioverse on emsenn.net

item.title: Decision: four-group directory structure with twelve cross-cutting types
item.description:
Context

A knowing semioverse needs a vocabulary of cross-cutting directory types that can appear within any Thing. The repository had accumulated ad-hoc types (terms/, concepts/, texts/, curricula/, disciplines/, schools/, topics/) without a principled account of why those types exist, how they relate, or what types are missing.

Research into knowledge organization systems, intellectual differentiation frameworks, epistemological structure of knowledge work, and process ontology was commissioned and produced six research texts, synthesized at information/disciplines/knowledge-systems/texts/research/ directory-organization-for-knowledge-systems.md.

item.title: Knowing Semioverse Specification
item.description:
This specification defines conformance requirements for implementing a knowing semioverse. A knowing semioverse is an agential semioverse (already conforming to the base agential semioverse specifications) that additionally implements the four-group directory organization described in the knowing-semioverse index.

1. Normative scope

This specification IS normative about:

  • Which twelve directory types are standard cross-cutting subdirectories
  • What content belongs in each type
  • The grouping of those twelve into four functional groups

This specification IS NOT normative about:
